Job Role Description: The Ranger Team Leader plays a pivotal role in leading and supporting a team of Rangers in providing exceptional experiences for visitors to Victoria's Surf Coast & Great Ocean Road region, including its parks and reserves. As a Ranger Team Leader, you are responsible for leading the on-ground management of GORCAPA's estate, consistent with organisational priorities, policies and procedures, ensuring that people can enjoy the outdoors and protecting the things that make our parks and reserves so special. You will lead a team in delivering a variety of projects and services including (but not limited to): working with Traditional Owners in joint management and other partnership arrangements conservation program delivery (such as pest plants and animals, threatened species) visitor infrastructure and asset construction and maintenance (such as roads, walking tracks, day visitor areas and campgrounds) compliance and enforcement activities the delivery of education programs engagement with the community, licensed tour operators and other commercial operations, and volunteers. Ranger Team Leaders are also encouraged to participate in Victoria’s fire and emergency management arrangements as part of Forest Fire Management Victoria (if applicable)
Job Role Skills and Experience: 1. Qualifications and experience • Relevant qualifications and skills for the management of GORCAPA's estate, such as environmental science, natural resource management, conservation and land management, outdoor education, community engagement, or a relevant trade. • Demonstrated experience and a record of achievement working as a Ranger or in an equivalent natural resource management role, with proven ability to supervise staff and deliver complex on ground services and projects. • Current Victorian driver’s licence. 2. Communication and interpersonal skills • Ability to give clear direction, provide constructive feedback, and motivate staff delivering works under your supervision. • Strong interpersonal skills and excellent verbal communication, including the ability to explain concepts to people of different backgrounds and to engage constructively with stakeholders. • Proven ability to prepare clear written communications, including work plans, reports, and correspondence. 3. Supervision and teamwork • Demonstrated ability to supervise and support staff, allocate workloads, and manage budgets and other resources to deliver agreed outcomes. • Demonstrated ability to foster a collaborative and respectful team culture that promotes safety, inclusion, and high performance. • Demonstrated commitment to high standards of integrity, accountability and professionalism in the workplace. 4. Problem solving and continuous improvement • Proven ability to use professional judgement, experience and initiative to resolve operational and service delivery issues and to realise opportunities for improvement. • Capacity to implement operational improvements that enhance safety, efficiency and effectiveness in service and project delivery. 5. Community engagement • Demonstrated capacity to work effectively with Traditional Owners, volunteers, contractors, community groups and park users in the delivery of park management services. • Proven ability to represent GORCAPA positively and build constructive relationships with stakeholders. 6. Accountability and reliability • Proven ability to work with autonomy and take responsibility for the quality, timeliness, and accuracy of your own work and that of your team.

Loading and unloading waste materials from residential and commercial properties onto the waste collection vehicle. Ensuring compliance with all health and safety regulations while performing duties, including wearing appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). Engaging positively with members of the public, addressing any inquiries or concerns regarding waste management services. Participating in training sessions to improve skills related to waste management practices and safety protocols. This role requires a proactive approach to teamwork, a commitment to public service, and an understanding of environmental sustainability principles. Demonstrated experience in working collaboratively within a team environment. Experience operating heavy vehicles or machinery, with relevant certifications preferred. Proven track record of adhering to safety protocols in operational activities. Experience dealing with the public, particularly in service-oriented roles.

Job Role Description: Faith Basis of Appointment We see the appointment of staff to Trinity Christian School as a ministry calling. The ethos, aims and objects of a Christian School are based on: > acceptance of the Lordship of Christ; and > acceptance of the Bible as the revealed Word of God Within Trinity Christian School these are expounded in the Doctrinal Basis. The Doctrinal Basis does not exhaust the extent of the beliefs of the School and its members. The Bible itself, as the inspired and infallible Word of God, that speaks with final authority concerning truth, morality, and the proper conduct of mankind, is the sole and final source of all that the School and its members believe. You must support the School in these areas and set an example of personal conduct and behaviour consistent with the School’s beliefs and ethos as outlined in documents made available to prospective employees prior to interview. Any appointment is conditional upon staff maintaining and demonstrating a continuing firm personal belief and lifestyle consistent with the School’s Doctrinal Basis and the other documents provided to staff, this being an essential condition of the appointment. A Learning Centred Christian School At Trinity Christian School we endeavour to make the link between our faith, what we do and how we teach. We call our students and school community to grow “loving hearts, enquiring minds and generous spirits”. At Trinity, how we teach must help our students in becoming who they were created to be in Christ. How we teach should also bring a Kingdom perspective, set students’ minds on things above, deliver meaning and show purpose. The pedagogy in our classrooms at Trinity should engage enquiring minds, build questioning, allow for grappling, exploring and reasoning. The students we teach should be given opportunities to grow and serve, so they may see who they are becoming and their purpose. As we undertake to grow our learners in both faith and learning, we must build in them the capacity to do so. As stated in our Strategic Priorities for 2024 – 2027, we have several key areas of focus that we are working toward: Christian Faith and Growth The spiritual nurturing of all individuals in the Christian faith, within a caring community of students, staff and families united by vision, mission and values. A passionate focus on realising the transformative power of the Gospel of Jesus, developing Biblical knowledge, and understanding and fulfilling the purpose of a Christian life. Excellence in Education and Learning All students actively engaged, growing and thriving as learners in a learning centred school. Parent partnership and student agency enhanced and supported. A commitment to educational excellence with the creation and support of new ways of learning, varied student pathways and models of education, to meet the needs of each learner. Community and Belonging A movement by all students, staff and families from joining and attending the school to belonging and actively investing in the Trinity community. Connection and relationship developed and strengthened with those in the School’s history as well as future community members. Connecting with the World and Shaping Tomorrow Ongoing design and formation of our 2030 learning environment for the community now and in the future. A growth in our reach and impact on Christian Education nationally and globally, including shaping a reputation as a centre for staffing development and excellence. Benefits of Working at Trinity Christian School Trinity Christian School is an employer that promotes staff excellence and values the benefit that our professional team bring to the school. In addition to the highly competitive remuneration and conditions as set out in the MEA we offer the following additional benefits to our staff across the school including: > Attractive salary packaging arrangements. > School fee concessions. > Generous annual Staff Development Award program. > Access to an Employee Assistance Program. > Access to annual influenza vaccinations. Other Essential Requirements Employment Conditions Employment will be subject to the conditions of the current ACT Christian School General Staff Multi-Enterprise Agreement 2021 or its replacement. The salary and classification for this position will be dependent on the qualifications and experience of the successful applicant. A Committed, Practicing Christian Applicants must be able to demonstrate a personal faith and commitment to the Lord Jesus Christ and a lifestyle consistent with that faith, including an active commitment to a Christian fellowship. Right to Work In Australia Applicants must be Australian Citizens or hold a current and valid working visa. The School will not consider sponsorship nor support a visa application process. Please do not submit an application if you cannot meet this requirement. Working With Vulnerable People Check In keeping with the School’s policy and legislative requirements, any offer of employment is subject to you providing a satisfactory Working With Vulnerable People check prior to commencing employment and maintaining this for the duration of your employment. Acknowledgement The requirements of this job description are intended to describe the general nature and responsibility of the work in this role. These statements are not to be construed as an exhaustive list of all duties, tasks and skills required of the job. Employees will also be required to follow any other job-related instructions and school policies, and to perform other job-related duties requested by their Manager to support the School’s compliance with its legislative obligations. The Manager may, through consultation with the employee, vary the responsibilities of the position temporarily as required, but within the skills and responsibility levels appropriate to the position. Further Information All enquiries will be treated confidentially. Initial questions can be directed to the People & Culture Administrator, Katherine Boyd by phone on +61 2 6210 7500 or email employment@tcs.act.edu.au Applications are ongoing and are to be submitted electronically as a single PDF document as an attachment to the online application form. Trinity Christian School 34 McBryde Crescent Wanniassa ACT Australia 2903

Position Summary: This is a critical leadership role responsible for driving the end-to-end M&A process, from identifying and sourcing opportunities through to negotiation, due diligence, execution, and assisting with post-acquisition integration and long-term value creation, including eventual exit planning for the existing shareholders. Key Responsibilities: Proactively identify, source, and cultivate a pipeline of potential acquisition targets within the SMSF audit sector across Australia. Develop and maintain relationships with business brokers, advisors, and potential vendors. Screen and evaluate potential acquisition opportunities against strategic criteria. Identify other opportunities through breaking current “admin-audit pooling arrangements” or identifying auditors with independence risk in the market. Lead negotiations on transaction terms, including valuation, deal structure, key commercial points, and preliminary agreements (e.g., Heads of Agreement, Letters of Intent). Develop and present compelling acquisition proposals. Oversee and coordinate the due diligence process, including financial, operational, legal, and regulatory (audit compliance) aspects. Liaise with internal teams and external advisors where applicable (legal, accounting) to ensure thorough vetting of targets. Analyse findings and assess risks and opportunities associated with potential acquisitions. Manage the transaction execution phase, working closely with legal counsel to draft and finalise definitive agreements (e.g., Asset / Sale and Purchase Agreements). Coordinate all activities required to achieve successful transaction closing. Work collaboratively with the dedicated full-time Integration Manager to ensure a smooth handover of acquired businesses/books post-completion. Provide support and context during the initial integration phase as required. Contribute strategic insights and support activities related to the long-term planning and potential execution of an ultimate exit for the consolidated business. Provide regular, clear, and concise reporting on M&A pipeline activity, deal progress, and performance metrics to the Head of Strategy, CEO and Board. Manage relationships with key internal and external stakeholders throughout the M&A process. Performance Expectations: Identify opportunities that align with the company’s long-term goals. The ROI from completed transactions is in line with the strategic business plan. The ability to execute acquisitions efficiently and within the required timeline. Achieve projected synergies post-acquisition. Ensuring the satisfaction of all stakeholders, managing expectations, and delivering during the Merger & Acquisition process. The ability to travel on a regular basis.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Relevant tertiary qualifications (e.g., Finance, Business, Law, Accounting). Proven track record of success in Merger & Acquisition, corporate development, investment banking, or a similar role with demonstrable experience in executing transactions end-to-end. Experience in acquiring professional services firms, or similar. Strong financial modelling, analysis, and valuation skills. Expertise in due diligence methodologies and best practices. Exceptional negotiation and influencing skills. Familiarity with legal documentation common in Merger & Acquisition transactions. Ability to work autonomously, manage multiple priorities, and meet deadlines. Strategic thinker with strong commercial judgment. Results-oriented, proactive, and deal-driven mindset. High degree of professionalism, integrity, and discretion. Analytical and detail oriented. Collaborative and able to work effectively with various teams. Resilient and adaptable.

Developing and implementing curriculum strategies that align with the school's vision and educational standards. Monitoring student progress and performance, implementing interventions where necessary to ensure academic success. Facilitating communication between parents, staff, and students to promote a collaborative educational experience. Organising professional development opportunities for teachers to foster continuous improvement in pedagogical approaches. Overseeing student welfare initiatives, ensuring a safe and supportive environment for all learners. Coordinating extracurricular activities to enrich the student experience beyond the classroom setting. Managing budgets associated with the senior school, ensuring resources are allocated effectively to meet educational goals. The Head of Senior plays an essential role in shaping the future leaders of tomorrow by providing direction, inspiration, and support within Faith Christian School's commitment to excellence in education.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Required skills Strong leadership and management abilities Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Strategic planning and organisational skills Proficient in conflict resolution and problem-solving Ability to inspire and motivate staff and students Knowledge of educational policies and procedures Competence in budget management and financial oversight Required experiences A minimum of 5 years' experience in a senior leadership role within an educational setting Demonstrated success in curriculum development and implementation Experience in staff recruitment, training, and development Proven track record of improving student outcomes Experience with stakeholder engagement, including parents, community members, and governing bodies Familiarity with data analysis for school performance evaluation

Job Role Description: The Chief Information Officer (CIO) is responsible for developing and implementing the organizations technology strategy to meet business goals and objectives. This includes ensuring the security and reliability of information systems, networks, and data. The CIO will manage the IT department, including hiring, training, and performance management of staff members. The CIO will evaluate and recommend new technology solutions to improve efficiency and productivity. The CIO will also collaborate with other department heads to align technology initiatives with overall business goals.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Strategic planning and decision-making skills for aligning technology with business goals Strong leadership and management skills for overseeing technology teams and departments Knowledge of emerging technologies and trends in the tech industry Project management skills for planning and executing technology projects effectively Budgeting and cost control skills for managing technology investments and expenses Excellent communication skills for interacting with stakeholders and explaining technical concepts to non-technical individuals Cybersecurity skills for identifying and mitigating cyber threats and ensuring data privacy and protection Data analysis skills for using data to inform business decisions and improve operational efficiency Vendor management skills for negotiating contracts and managing relationships with technology vendors Change management skills for leading and managing technology-related changes within the organization.

Job Role Description: Purpose The primary objective of this role is to provide exceptional service and technical support to customers using Valley and other brand centre pivot irrigation systems, as well as related water delivery products. This includes installation, diagnostics, maintenance, and troubleshooting of systems across a wide geographic area. The Service Technician will work collaboratively with the Irrigation Sales/Service Operations team and other internal departments to resolve technical issues for new and existing customers, ensuring high-quality service delivery and promoting customer satisfaction. This role plays a key part in driving the growth of Emmetts' irrigation business through consistent customer support and the generation of revenue from parts and service sales. Responsibilities The following is a summary list of duties required for the position as described above: Technical Service & Support Provide diagnostics, repair, and maintenance services for Valley and non-Valley center pivot and lateral irrigation systems. Install, troubleshoot, and support irrigation systems and water delivery products for both new and existing customers. Upgrade and replace aging components, ensuring system compatibility and functionality. Identify and explain the function of irrigation system components with confidence and clarity. Demonstrate strong operational troubleshooting skills and the ability to identify effective solutions. Customer Service & Business Growth Deliver exceptional customer service and ongoing support to maintain long-term customer relationships. Advise customers on repair and replacement options in alignment with system capabilities and requirements. Assist in promoting and selling aftermarket service and parts solutions to support business growth. Represent Emmetts professionally in all interactions, promoting a positive brand image in the market. Team & Operational Support Collaborate with the Irrigation Leads and team members to ensure timely resolution of technical issues. Support the development of a cohesive team environment focused on customer success and business outcomes. Work with external contractors and service providers to ensure successful project outcomes. Monitor and report customer feedback to support continuous improvement and customer satisfaction. Administration & Reporting Accurately track and report service hours, travel, and parts usage. Complete administrative tasks and reports as required for assigned jobs and service calls. Maintain a clean, safe, and orderly work environment, including company tools, equipment, and vehicles. Other Duties Provide service support across a large geographic area, based out of the Murray Bridge branch. Participate in training and development programs to enhance skills and technical knowledge. Undertake additional duties as directed, within the scope of skills and experience.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Qualifications & Experience Essential Proven problem-solving and decision-making skills. Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical information clearly. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite. Current Australian driver’s licence. MR or HR Truck Licence Australian citizenship or appropriate working rights. Ability to remain calm and professional in high-pressure or challenging situations. Willingness to travel frequently, including extended stays within South Australia and interstate. Demonstrated ability to work independently with minimal supervision. Commitment to seeing tasks through to completion. Willingness to obtain a B-Class electrical licence if not already held. Trade qualification in mechanical, electrical, plumbing, or related field (e.g., fitter and turner, mechanic, plumber, electrician). Desirable Prior experience in the agricultural irrigation industry. Familiarity with centre pivot irrigation systems. Experience within the broader agricultural sector. Advanced knowledge of irrigation system compatibility and software.

Job Role Description: The Position The Planned Activity Worker (PAG) will support HACC eligible Aboriginal elders, people with a disability and/or their carers, to stay connected to community, country and culture through social interaction and gatherings. Key Responsibilities Receive referrals from Intake, make contact with client to engage in planned activities group, Develop and facilitate approved planned activities for Elders, HACC clients and ABI clients, Complete six-monthly medical care plans and goal setting plans for each PAG client, Ensure that the Summary Medical Care Register is updated after reviews and copies given to clients, Facilitate Care Plan meetings before every activity to ensure safety of each participant

The purpose of the New Business Lead is to develop a robust sales pipeline of potential opportunities and convert these into live projects for UAP, driving revenue and growth in Australia. The New Business Lead will establish and nurture long-term relationships with artists, architects, developers, cultural consultants and other consultants to promote UAPs vision and capabilities in both consultancy and manufacturing. This role requires a strategic, commercial mindset and understanding of the complex requirements of contract negotiation, project delivery, capabilities, and systems. Key to the roles success will be your drive and passion for sales, and interest in art and design. Staying abreast of industry and market trends will assist you and the business in succeeding.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Duties and Responsibilities Sales and Prospecting Identify and prospect potential new clients to increase business for UAP. Leverage existing networks and build new relationships to grow a strong client base. Conduct market research to identify and engage decision-makers within potential client organisations. Actively promote UAPs capabilities and vision in delivering small and mid to large-scale artwork projects. Pipeline Development and Management Generate, maintain and convert a strong pipeline of projects, focusing on the New South Wales market, and providing support to the business as required. Prepare and deliver high-quality submissions for project opportunities to meet sales and revenue targets.. Client Relationship Management Develop and maintain strong relationships with new and existing clients, ensuring continuous engagement and support. Handle and resolve any barriers during the sales process, ensuring positive outcomes for the client and artist. Present innovative ideas and opportunities to clients to enhance engagement and secure new projects. Proposal and Contract Management Lead the creation and submission of proposals, ensuring timely follow-up and conversion into sales. Negotiate contract terms to align with client expectations and UAPs capabilities. Manage and document all project proposals and agreements to ensure accuracy and completeness. Market and Industry Engagement Attend industry functions, association events, and conferences to gather market insights and trends. Provide feedback to the team on market conditions and creative trends to inform strategic decisions. Strategic Sales Initiatives Identify, pursue and convert strategic opportunities that align with UAPs business objectives. Develop unique selling propositions and differentiators based on market analysis and client needs. Ensure all sales and revenue targets are met in accordance with the annual budget.
